title: isgpuarray
summary: Test whether values are gpuArray handles in MATLAB and RunMat.

gpu_support.notes: Answers using value metadata; no device gather is required.

description: `tf = isgpuarray(x)` returns true when `x` is a gpuArray handle and false for other value types, following MATLAB semantics.
behaviors: gpuArray values return `true` without gathering device buffers.
Numeric, logical, complex, string, character, struct, cell, and object inputs return `false`.
The result is always a logical scalar.

examples.description: Host arrays are not gpuArray values

faqs.question: Does `isgpuarray` gather data back to the host?
faqs.answer: No. It inspects value metadata and returns a logical scalar without triggering a device transfer.

faqs.question: What happens if no acceleration provider is registered?
faqs.answer: `isgpuarray` still reports accurately for values that are already gpuArray handles. If a value is not a gpuArray handle, it returns `false`.

gpu_residency: RunMat keeps data on the GPU when a provider is active, and `isgpuarray` lets you verify residency boundaries in MATLAB-style code.
gpu_behavior: The builtin only checks whether the value is a gpuArray handle. It never gathers device buffers or inspects the underlying data contents.
